description:
# Open question

`bl sync` and `bl prime` walkthroughs.

Both invoke the full plugin chain with their respective verbs. Not tracker-exclusive — every plugin gets a turn.

- `bl sync`: tracker fetches and fast-forwards (or aborts on conflict, per "remote wins" rule). Other plugins may refresh their caches.
- `bl prime`: comprehensive setup/refresh. Other plugins may bootstrap state, fetch external tickets, etc.

Spec:
- Payload shape for these verbs (no `command`, no `previous_state` — what's there?)
- cwd = operating/ (no change-attempt worktree)
- Failure semantics: rollback in reverse, same as state-change ops
- Contention: tracker's sync detects remote has moved → aborts → user re-runs (per the "remote wins, user resolves" rule)
- Pre-sync vs sync? Tracker pre-sync as a fast contention probe, then plugins do their work, then sync commits? Or simpler: just `sync`?

Resolve the verbs and shape.
title: Topic: bl sync and bl prime walkthroughs
